chain.thought: Explanation: Interpreting each premiers' score in goals.behinds (total points), we consider the goals (first number). Goals greater than 10 occurred in 2003 (14), 2004 (17), 2005 (19), 2006 (11), 2007 (14), 2008 (12), 2011 (11), 2012 (17), and 2013 (14). 2009 (9) and 2010 (8) are not more than 10. Thus, 9 premiers had a score more than ten.

judge_thought:
Explanation:
The table’s “score” for premiers is presented in the composite Australian rules format “goals.behinds (total points)”, which includes three distinct components. The question “how many premiers had a score that was more than ten?” is ambiguous because “score” could refer to goals, behinds, or total points. Each interpretation yields a different count (e.g., all total points exceed 10; most goals exceed 10; several behinds exceed 10), so the table does not allow a single, unambiguous numeric answer. Given this ambiguity inherent in the data presentation, returning “null” appropriately reflects that the count cannot be determined without further specification, aligning with the table and the reasoning process.
